msgs.text:
According to the current attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 124 is another attachment-only continuation. The required current-turn files are present, and I used only the attached mega_prompt.md, problem_statement.md, and turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md.

Turn 124 — attachment-only idempotence remains closed

The state is unchanged from Turn 123:(THEOREM_STATE,MEASURED_CERTIFICATE_D_STATE)=(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).The theorem coordinate remains closed. The problem asks whether there is a continuous isomorphismΦ:ΓS∼ΓH such thatπH∘Φ=πS,i.e. an isomorphism over the fixed quotient G. The attached sweep records the stronger F=P⋊G-level statement: there is a continuous over-G automorphism Φ:F=P⋊G⟶F=P⋊GwithΦ(rS−1)=rH.

Since rS and rS−1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this descends to

F/⟨⟨rS⟩⟩F≅G

F/⟨⟨rH⟩⟩F,henceΓS≅GΓH.This is exactly the requested over-G isomorphism condition.The frozen proof chain remains the same uniform certificate chain:H1 -Nielsen equivalence+residual restricted-Lie induction+normalized Koszul–Witt coefficient induction+D.1–D.4+finite filtered Nielsen compactness⟹ΓS≅GΓH.The compactness mechanism remains the corrected filtered-Nielsen one: for coefficient-and-degree truncations PN,J, the finite sets SN,J consist of filtered Nielsen/cocycle data, not arbitrary quotient automorphisms; each finite datum has invertible first Magnus layer and gives a finite over-G automorphism; a compatible inverse limit gives Φ:F→F with Φ(rS−1)=rH.No structural case should be reopened under the present inputs. The non-Ef pieces are unit-controlled; the residual Ef,mod2,s=1 tower is closed by the induction Cm=Lm; the Ef-coefficient tower is closed after exact normalization of the local row to (2,t) and the Koszul–Witt cascade; and finite filtered Nielsen compactness globalizes the finite solutions.The strict measured Certificate D coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments are only the prompt/problem/history set; they do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D.1–D.4 artifacts such as independent scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs. Therefore the strict measured Certificate D gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. The attached sweep repeatedly separates the theorem coordinate from this external reproducibility gate.

Thus the attachment-only transition rule applies again:{mega_prompt.md,problem_statement.md,turns_history__q2_candidate_iso.md}⟹(ΓS≅GΓH, NO_MEASURED_INPUT).

ESTABLISHED: Turn 124 is idempotent under the same attachment-only conditions as Turn 123. The theorem coordinate remains ΓS≅GΓH, via an over-G automorphism Φ:F→F satisfying Φ(rS−1)=rH. The strict measured Certificate D coordinate remains NO_MEASURED_INPUT.

GAP: No structural mathematical gap is reopened. The only remaining gap is external reproducibility: no genuine object-level measured nonlinear D.1–D.4 artifacts are attached in this turn.

NEXT: Keep the obstruction sweep closed. The only non-idempotent next move is to attach genuine measured D.1–D.4 scripts, matrices, pivots, or measured-output logs not derived from the history, then run the strict gate as PASS or FAIL.[TURN124-ATTACHMENT-ONLY-IDEMPOTENT-GATE]
